using-select-first.label: Using SELECT first
using-select-first.summary: Learn how and why to use SELECT statements to preview affected rows before running UPDATE or DELETE commands, ensuring safer data modifications in the inventory_items table.

code-preview-select-before-update.title: Preview rows before updating
code-preview-select-before-update.prompt: You want to update the status to 'inactive' for all inventory items in the 'Accessories' category that are currently 'draft'. Write a SELECT statement to preview which rows would be affected.
code-preview-select-before-update.hint: Focus on the concept being tested.

help.concept: Think about the role or idea being tested rather than repeating answer wording.
help.hint_1: Eliminate choices or interpretations that do not match the task.
help.hint_2: Choose the concept that best fits what the exercise is asking you to do.

code-preview-select-before-delete.title: Preview rows before deleting
code-preview-select-before-delete.prompt: Before deleting all test items from the inventory, write a SELECT statement to preview which rows will be deleted.
code-preview-select-before-delete.hint: Focus on the concept being tested.

help.concept: Think about the role or idea being tested rather than repeating answer wording.
help.hint_1: Eliminate choices or interpretations that do not match the task.
help.hint_2: Choose the concept that best fits what the exercise is asking you to do.

code-update-after-preview.title: Update after preview
code-update-after-preview.prompt: After previewing, update the status to 'inactive' for all inventory items in the 'Accessories' category that are currently 'draft'. Write the UPDATE statement.
code-update-after-preview.hint: Focus on the SQL task being asked for, not on copying final query text.

help.concept: Build the query from the operation the exercise is testing.
help.hint_1: Think about which clauses or functions are required for the task.
help.hint_2: Construct the query based on what result the exercise expects, not by repeating exact solution wording.

multi-why-select-first.title: Why use SELECT before UPDATE or DELETE?
multi-why-select-first.prompt: Which of the following are reasons to run a SELECT statement before executing an UPDATE or DELETE? (Choose all that apply.)
multi-why-select-first.hint: Think about safety, accuracy, and preventing mistakes.

help.concept: Running a SELECT first helps you verify which rows will be affected and avoid accidental data changes.
help.hint_1: Previewing helps you confirm your WHERE clause and avoid mistakes.
help.hint_2: SELECT lets you see the affected rows, catch errors, and prevent unwanted changes.

fill-blank-select-preview-purpose.title: Purpose of SELECT preview
fill-blank-select-preview-purpose.prompt: Choose the best value for the first missing blank in the statement.
fill-blank-select-preview-purpose.hint: What does the SELECT statement show you?

help.concept: A SELECT preview lets you check which rows match your criteria before making changes.
help.hint_1: It helps you check or confirm the affected rows.
help.hint_2: The SELECT lets you the missing term the rows that would be changed or deleted.

sketch-why-select-preview.title: Why Preview with SELECT?
sketch-why-select-preview.bodyMarkdown:
Before making changes to your data with UPDATE or DELETE, it's a best practice to first run a SELECT query with the same WHERE condition. This lets you see exactly which rows will be affected, helping you avoid accidental changes or deletions. For example:

```sql
-- Preview which items are marked as test items
SELECT id, name, is_test FROM inventory_items WHERE is_test = 1;
```

If the preview looks correct, you can then safely proceed with your UPDATE or DELETE statement using the same WHERE clause.

sketch-select-update-workflow.title: Safe UPDATE Workflow
sketch-select-update-workflow.bodyMarkdown:
Suppose you want to change the status of all test items from 'draft' to 'inactive'. First, preview the affected rows:

```sql
SELECT id, name, status FROM inventory_items WHERE is_test = 1 AND status = 'draft';
```

If the results match your expectations, then run:

```sql
UPDATE inventory_items SET status = 'inactive' WHERE is_test = 1 AND status = 'draft';
```

This two-step process helps prevent accidental updates.

sketch-select-delete-workflow.title: Safe DELETE Workflow
sketch-select-delete-workflow.bodyMarkdown:
Deleting data is irreversible, so always preview first! For example, to remove all test items:

```sql
SELECT id, name FROM inventory_items WHERE is_test = 1;
```

After confirming the preview, you can run:

```sql
DELETE FROM inventory_items WHERE is_test = 1;
```

This habit protects you from deleting the wrong rows.
